def split_words(txt):
    '''
    For a given string, the function attempts to split it into words using whitespace as the delimiter. When whitespace is absent, commas are used instead. If there are no commas, the function returns the count of lowercase letters that have an odd ord value, with ord('a') being 0.
    Examples
    split_words("Hello world!") ➞ ["Hello", "world!"]
    split_words("Hello,world!") ➞ ["Hello", "world!"]
    split_words("abcdef") == 3 
    '''
